findings.evidence: `SemigroupGroupPullback.lean:64-75` defines the helper; Mathlib provides the generic hom-class subtraction lemma via `@[to_additive] theorem map_div` in `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Hom/Defs.lean:452-457`.
findings.fix: Delete the private helper and use Mathlib's hom-class `map_sub` directly in the `simpa` calls, e.g. `simpa [map_sub] using hK` where needed.
findings.issue: `map_sub_of_addHomClass` manually derives `map_zero`, `map_neg`, and subtraction preservation for an `AddHomClass`; this duplicates the standard generic `map_sub` API and leaves the main `simpa` proofs depending on a local brittle algebra proof.

summary: One proof helper re-proves standard hom-class subtraction algebra manually, making the new pullback proofs less robust than the existing Mathlib API.
